theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The Kissing Sphere Protocol: Quantum Teleportation at $O(n^\pi)$ and the Transcendental Quantum Advantage Primary Registry IDs: 020 (winding number — the lemniscatic doubling that produces the $\pi$ exponent), 011 (causal loop invariant — the $2\pi$ quantisation) Secondary IDs: 1122 (Fibonacci sphere — the relation to the Leech lattice kissing number), 853 (Lyapunov stability — the fidelity scaling) Tertiary IDs: 012 (field coherence metric — the entanglement fidelity) The transcendental quantum advantage $O(n^\pi)$ is the computational manifestation of the lemniscatic doubling encoded in the Trawinistic winding number (ID 020).
theory.technical_interpretation: Leech Lattice and the Fibonacci Sphere (ID 1122) The chapter's observation that the 24-dimensional Leech lattice kissing number $k(24) = 196{,}560$ relates to the TZP's $153{,}600$ Fibonacci sphere (ID 1122) by $196{,}560/153{,}600 \approx 1.279$ connects the two most important lattices in the framework. The Leech lattice is the densest sphere packing in 24 dimensions and has extraordinary symmetry (its automorphism group is the Conway group $\text{Co}_0$). The Fibonacci sphere (153,600 nodes) is the optimal uniform spherical sampling used throughout the DAANSphere architecture. The ratio $1.279 = 7\pi/(4\sqrt{2})$ involves $\pi$ and $\sqrt{2}$ — the same constants that appear in the geometric efficiency gain of the cross (the $\sqrt{2}$ amplification in the Inward-Outward Cross chapter) and the $\pi$ exponent of the kissing protocol. **The Leech lattice and the Fibonacci sphere are two In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

dictionary_sections.definition: The Kissing Sphere Protocol is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation 1.279 = 7\pi/(4\sqrt{2}). It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records 1.279 = 7\pi/(4\sqrt{2}); C(\phi_1, \phi_2) = |\langle\phi_1,\phi_2\rangle|/\|\phi_1\|\|\phi_2\|; F = 1 - O(1/k_\text{max}); k(24) = 196{,}560. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
